title: Venice AI raises $65M as demand grows for privacy-first model access
friendlyTitle: Venice AI raises money for private AI access
summary: TechCrunch reported that Venice AI raised a $65 million Series A at a $1 billion valuation. The platform offers access to hundreds of models with a privacy-focused architecture and says it is already profitable with more than $70 million in annualized run-rate revenue.
friendlySummary: Venice AI raised $65 million and says it offers private access to many AI models.
whyItMatters: Privacy is becoming a mainstream buying criterion for AI tools, not just a niche concern. Venice’s growth suggests some users will pay for model choice, fewer data-retention worries, and more control over how their prompts are handled.
friendlyWhyItMatters: People and businesses are paying more attention to what happens to their data when they use AI tools.
